ab: Tduwa ku smiling sunan hug?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Iq! Smruwa ku.
ch: B:可以，我很願意。
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Tduwa ku smiling sunan hug?
ch: A:我可以訪問你嗎?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Iya, ini keegu ka klaan mu.
ch: B:不要，我所知道的不多。
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: Niqan jiyax su babaw nkan ska hidaw hug? Kmusa ku qmita sunan.
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Iq! Tgaun misu sapah.
ch: B:好，我會在家等你。
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: Niqan jiyax su kusun hug? Kmusa ku smiling sunan quri kari snruan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你阿美族傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the story. *[Amis] Please change to your respective tribe.

ab: Musa ku dgiyaq daya ka kusun, ini ku eniq sapah wah!
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: Qnbahang mu o klaun su bi ka endaan rudan Seejiq sbiyaw, mkmsilng ku sunan.
ch: A:我聽說您對賽克德族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I've heard that you're very knowledgeable about the traditional customs of the Secedes and would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Uxay egu ka klaan mu, ida mu rngagun kana ka klaan.
ch: B:我了解的不多，我會把知道的說出來回答。
en: B: I don't know much, I'll answer with what I know.

ab: Tduwa ku Ruing hug? Psklxan mu matas hici.
ch: A:(請問)我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Iq! Ini huya!
ch: B:好的，沒問題。
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Piya ka hngkwasan su dhug?
ch: A:(請問)您今年幾歲了?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Mngari kmxalan mspac ka hngkwasan mu da.
ch: B:我已經98歲了。
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: Mbiyax su bi qtaan.
ch: A:您真年輕。
en: A: You're so young.

ab: Mbiyax inu da, rudan ku da.
ch: B:沒有啦!我已經老了。
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: Pnaah su inu?
ch: A:(請問)您來自哪裡?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Pnaah ku alang Pulan.
ch: B:我來自布嵐部落。
en: B: I come from the Buran tribe.

ab: Seejiq balay uri ka qrijil su hug?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是賽德克族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Sedek?

ab: Uxay! Bunun ka hiya.
ch: B:不，她是布農族人。
en: B: No, she's a Bunun.

ab: Mkla su rmngaw kari dha hug?
ch: A:您會說他們的語言?
en: A: You speak their language?

ab: Mkla ku rmngaw kari Truku ni bunun.
ch: B: 我會說太魯閣語和布農語。
en: B: I speak Telugu and Bunun.

ab: Qrasun balay ka miyah smiling sunan, rngagi cikuh ka quri Gaya ssaan tmsamac hug?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Iq! Mbiyax ku siida o musa ku bi lmiqu.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Mhuway su balay, egu balay ka tnegsa su knan jiyax sayang.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天真的指導我很多。
en: A: Thank you, you really guided me a lot today.

ab: Ida balay bi hnici kari rdrudan sbiyaw ka snruan mu nii.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Dhuq hini ka ssiling mu sunan. Mhuway su bi balay.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Ini huya! Tduwa ta pprngaw na.
ch: B:沒有關係, 我們還可以聊。
en: B: It's okay, we can still talk.

ab: Musa ku han, babaw do mkmqbahang ku egu snru kari na.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Han! Kmhuway musa ha.
ch: B:好喔!請慢走。
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
